name: Supriya Sankaran
bio: Supriya is the co-founder of Agami and of PUCAR itself, working to transform how every Indian experiences justice. Through the Agami Prize, Agami Challenges, and AgamiShaala she has spent years unleashing changemakers in law and justice. Before Agami she was a Director at Ashoka Innovators for the Public, and began her career as a corporate lawyer.

name: Varun Hemachandran
bio: Varun is a systems thinker and civic technologist working at the intersection of courts, AI, and the people caught between them. A Senior Curator at PUCAR, he previously co-led OpenNyAI, the collaborative mission building AI public goods for justice in India, and has curated communities at Agami since 2020. Earlier he founded Talking Earth, a civic-data project crowdsourcing tree censuses in Bengaluru.

name: Ayushi Singhal
bio: Ayushi co-leads PUCAR, where she has anchored the mission since 2022. A practising advocate at the High Courts of Bombay and Rajasthan, she was previously a disputes associate at Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas, clerked for Justice D.Y. Chandrachud at the Supreme Court of India, and assisted the Srikrishna Committee on arbitration reform at Vidhi. She studied law at NUJS Kolkata, where she also taught tort and constitutional law.

name: Mehul Sehgal
bio: Mehul is a Program Lead at PUCAR, which he joined after nearly four years at Samagra. There he helped set up the pilot 24x7 ON Court in Kerala, building digital public goods and improving legal processes around the court experience. He previously worked with KPMG and FICCI.

name: Aditi Phadnis
bio: Aditi is the Product Lead at PUCAR, driving DRISTI, the open-source dispute resolution platform at the heart of the collective's work. Before this she built population-scale public systems at Samagra and worked at J.P. Morgan India. She holds an MBA from the University of Oxford.

name: Sathyajith M S
bio: Sathyajith is a Program Associate at PUCAR, based in Kochi, working on transforming litigant experiences end to end through process, policy and technology. Before PUCAR he spent over two years as a political consultant at Varahe Analytics, working across the 2024 Kerala, Odisha and Maharashtra election campaigns. He holds a Master's in Public Policy from NLSIU.

name: Abhiram Rajilan
bio: Abhiram is a product designer and design researcher at PUCAR, bringing ethnography and service design to how litigants experience courts. Trained in Strategic Design Management at the National Institute of Design, he has designed UX at Fractal, shaped design-ops processes at Ather Energy, and consulted for products across mobility, sport and health.

name: Anshumanth Rao
bio: Anshumanth is a Product Manager at PUCAR, having moved over from eGov Foundation where he worked on the collective's dispute resolution tooling as an Associate Product Manager. Before turning to public technology he was an analyst at Goldman Sachs and a software engineer at Cisco. An ISB Dean's Lister, he works on the product backbone of the DRISTI platform.

name: Garvita Gupta
bio: Garvita works on governance and justice innovation at PUCAR, which she joined after interning with Agami. She holds a Master's in Public Policy from NLSIU and studied political science at LSR, Delhi. At NLSIU she was a research assistant on a GenAI consumer-grievance project with Meta, IIT Bombay and the Department of Consumer Affairs, and she has been a fellow at Climate Collective and a tech-policy intern at The Dialogue.

name: Atul Gupta
bio: Atul is PUCAR's Chief Technology Officer and an Innovator in Residence at Agami, leading the technology strategy behind DRISTI, the open, modular and interoperable platform powering the collective's court transformation work. A cloud architect by training, he has spent years designing digital public infrastructure.
